statusLine: EU fuel security remains under pressure with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, while oil prices have surged 52.5% since late February.
fullAnalysis:
The European Union's fuel security picture shows concerning strain across multiple countries, with 19 of 27 member states currently maintaining critical reserve levels in at least one fuel category. Romania faces the most severe shortfall with just 33.2 days of petrol reserves and 32.4 days of diesel—both far below the mandatory 90-day minimum. Poland and Luxembourg also show particularly weak positions across all three fuel types.

Jet fuel reserves present the most widespread vulnerability, with 17 of 27 countries holding critical levels below 90 days. This includes major economies like Germany (61.3 days), France (59.6 days), and Italy (68.1 days). Diesel reserves are under pressure in 14 countries, while petrol shows the strongest overall position with only 11 countries below the safety threshold.

The security challenge is compounded by dramatically elevated oil prices, with Brent crude reaching $110.5 per barrel—a 52.5% surge since the late-February Middle East escalation began. Average pump prices reflect this pressure, with petrol at €1.777 per liter and diesel at €1.913 per liter across the EU. Despite these headwinds, the EU maintains aggregate reserves above minimum thresholds: petrol at 103.8 days, diesel at 96.2 days, and jet fuel at 90.1 days.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries maintain critical reserve levels in at least one fuel type, indicating widespread strain
Romania shows the most severe shortfall with petrol and diesel reserves at just 33 days—far below the 90-day requirement
Jet fuel presents the broadest vulnerability with 17 countries below critical thresholds, including Germany, France, and Italy
Oil prices have surged 52.5% since late February to $110.5 per barrel, adding financial pressure alongside supply concerns
EU aggregate reserves remain above mandatory minimums despite individual country shortfalls
